name: Irauregi-Alonsotegi — circular por la Ruta del Peñas Blancas (PR-BI 101)
region_intro: Hiking around Alonsotegi offers access to a diverse natural landscape characterized by mountainous terrain and river corridors. The municipality is situated amidst prominent ranges such as Ganekogorta (998 meters), Pagasarri (673 meters), and Sierra Sasiburu (up to 500 meters), providing varied elevations and panoramic views. The Cadagua River flows through the area, contributing to its natural beauty, while ecosystems include oak groves, beech forests, and expansive meadows. The region's altitude ranges from 50 to 1,000 meters above sea level, creating steep slopes and dramatic scenery for hikers. Best hiking trails around Alonsotegi The most popular hiking route is Irauregi-Alonsotegi — circular por la Ruta del Peñas Blancas (PR-BI 101), a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes about 3 hours to complete. This moderate route leads to the Sasiburu pass, offering views of the Kadagua valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hike from Zaramillo — circular via the Ruta de las Minas and Seles (PR-BI 100.2), a difficult 11.0 miles (17.7 km) path. This trail follows the slopes of the Ganekogorta mountain range, exploring old mines and historical fountains. Local hikers also love the Bilbao Green Ring: Arrastaleku and Arraiz Loop, a 6.9 miles (11.2 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 4 hours. Hiking around Alonsotegi is defined by rugged mountains, river corridors, and diverse ecosystems such as oak groves and beech forests. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easily accessible paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Alonsotegi are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Alonsotegi's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails can I find around Alonsotegi?
A: Based on komoot data, there are over 25 hiking trails available around Alonsotegi, catering to various skill levels. This includes 2 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 9 difficult routes, ensuring options for every hiker.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on hikes in Alonsotegi?
A: Alonsotegi offers a diverse landscape with mountainous terrain, river corridors, and varied elevations ranging from 50 to 1,000 meters. You'll find trails for all fitness levels, from easily accessible paths to more challenging routes with steep slopes and dramatic scenery. The region is characterized by prominent ranges like Ganekogorta, Pagasarri, and Sierra Sasiburu.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Alonsotegi?
A: Yes, Alonsotegi features several circular routes that allow you to explore the area's natural beauty. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1307961295/irauregi-alonsotegi-circular-por-la-ruta-del-penas-blancas-pr-bi-101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irauregi-Alonsotegi — circular por la Ruta del Peñas Blancas (PR-BI 101)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers impressive views of the Kadagua valley.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see while hiking in Alonsotegi?
A: Hiking in Alonsotegi allows you to discover unique historical and natural features. You can explore the 17th-century Pagasarri's Ice Houses, which were used to store snow, or visit several hermitages like San Antolín de Iráuregui. Natural highlights include the Cadagua River corridor, diverse ecosystems with oak and beech forests, and panoramic views from mountain ranges such as Ganekogorta and Pagasarri.

Q: Are there any specific trails that explore the mining history of the region?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1307075111/hike-from-zaramillo-circular-via-the-ruta-de-las-minas-and-seles-pr-bi-100-2" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike from Zaramillo — circular via the Ruta de las Minas and Seles (PR-BI 100.2)&lt;/a&gt; follows the slopes of the Ganekogorta mountain range, allowing you to explore old mines, ancient stones, and historical fountains, offering a glimpse into the area's rich mining past.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Alonsotegi?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Alonsotegi, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 190 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscape, the panoramic views from the mountains, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels. The combination of rugged mountains, lush forests, and historical landmarks makes for a compelling outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Alonsotegi?
A: Alonsotegi offers a range of trails, including easily accessible paths suitable for families. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the presence of diverse ecosystems, historical sites like hermitages, and the general beauty of the Cadagua River corridor provide engaging elements for all ages. It's recommended to check individual route details for distance and elevation to find the best fit for your family.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alonsotegi?
A: The region's diverse natural landscape makes it suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery with blooming flora or changing leaves. Summer can be warm but is also popular, while winter hiking is possible, especially on lower elevation trails, though conditions may vary on higher peaks.

Q: Can I find trails with impressive viewpoints in Alonsotegi?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous terrain of Alonsotegi, including ranges like Ganekogorta and Pagasarri, provides numerous opportunities for panoramic views. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1307961295/irauregi-alonsotegi-circular-por-la-ruta-del-penas-blancas-pr-bi-101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Peñas Blancas&lt;/a&gt; lead to passes offering impressive vistas of the Kadagua valley and surrounding peaks, including Bilbao.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or points of interest near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, you can find several attractions nearby. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5322343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Pagasarri&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ganekogorta Summit&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning views. Further afield, but still accessible, are cultural landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/335425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guggenheim Museum&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2911339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zubizuri Bridge&lt;/a&gt; in Bilbao, which can be combined with a hiking trip.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails in Alonsotegi?
A: The diverse ecosystems of Alonsotegi, including oak groves, beech forests, and expansive meadows, support a variety of wildlife. Nature enthusiasts might spot animals such as wild boars and roe deer, adding to the natural experience of hiking in the region.

Q: Is Alonsotegi suitable for hikers looking for a challenge?
A: Yes, Alonsotegi is a prime destination for trekking enthusiasts seeking more challenging routes. The region features several difficult trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1307075111/hike-from-zaramillo-circular-via-the-ruta-de-las-minas-and-seles-pr-bi-100-2" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike from Zaramillo — circular via the Ruta de las Minas and Seles (PR-BI 100.2)&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 11 miles with significant elevation gain, requiring good fitness. The steep slopes and varied elevations provide a rewarding challenge for experienced hikers.
